data.title: Tokyo Stocks Closes Higher On Tech Exuberance
data.description:
Tokyo stocks closed higher on Thursday, supported by gains in technology shares.

The benchmark Nikkei stock index, the 225-issue Nikkei Stock Average, ended up 307.00 points, or 0.46 percent, from Wednesday at 66,422.60.

The broader Topix index, meanwhile, finished 20.75 points, or 0.51 percent, higher at 4,053.88.

On the top-tier Prime Market, mining, bank and nonferrous metal issues were notable gainers.

Analysts said some heavyweight semiconductor and artificial intelligence-related shares rose after undergoing a correction phase, buoyed by signs of sustained strong demand for AI infrastructure in the United States.

However, the market’s upside was limited as rising Japanese government bond yields and higher crude oil futures fueled concerns over their potential impact on corporate earnings

data.title: Wall Street Slides As AI Spending Concerns And US$100 Oil Weigh On Markets
data.description:
Wall Street ended sharply lower on Thursday as investors grew increasingly concerned over rising artificial intelligence (AI) spending by major technology companies, while oil prices climbing above US$100 a barrel fuelled fresh inflation worries ahead of next week’s Federal Reserve meeting.

The Nasdaq Composite led losses, falling 553.21 points, or 2.15%, to 25,137.69. The S&P 500 dropped 90.66 points, or 1.21%, to 7,408.30, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average lost 506.93 points, or 0.97%, to close at 51,711.65.

Investor sentiment weakened after Alphabet and Tesla became the first of the “Magnificent Seven” technology companies to report quarterly earnings this season.

Alphabet shares fell 7% after the Google parent announced higher capital expenditure plans despite posting solid earnings, reviving concerns over mounting AI infrastructure costs. Tesla plunged 14.5% after reporting negative free cash flow for the first time in more than two years.

The sell-off intensified as Brent crude settled above US$100 per barrel for the first time since May, while US crude futures closed above US$92 amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. The rise followed fresh US air strikes on Iran and attacks on oil tankers in the Red Sea, heightening concerns over global energy supplies.

Higher oil prices also pushed US Treasury yields higher as investors worried renewed energy-driven inflation could limit the Federal Reserve’s ability to ease monetary policy. Markets, however, continue to expect the central bank to leave interest rates unchanged next week, with CME FedWatch indicating a roughly 64% probability of no change.

Despite broad market weakness, defence stocks outperformed. Lockheed Martin surged 10.5% after raising its 2026 sales and profit forecasts, while RTX gained 7.3% after upgrading its annual outlook on stronger demand for commercial aircraft maintenance and military systems.

The CBOE Volatility Index, widely known as Wall Street’s fear gauge, climbed to 18.7 after earlier touching its highest level in almost a month. Declining stocks outnumbered gainers by nearly three to one on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: HSIF Eyes 25,300 Resistance Amid Strong Technical Setup
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its positive outlook on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), saying the contract’s bullish structure remains intact despite a pullback in the evening session.

The research house noted that HSIF gained 294 points on Thursday to close at 25,210 points after trading between an intraday low of 24,863 points and a high of 25,290 points. During the evening session, however, the contract eased 325 points to around 24,885 points.

RHB Research said the relative strength index (RSI) remains above the 50% level, indicating that bullish momentum is still in play.

It added that the contract continues to trade above both its 20-day and 50-day simple moving average lines, giving buyers the technical advantage.

The research house is maintaining its long trading position initiated at 24,657 points on July 15 and has raised its stop-loss level to 24,200 points from 23,500 points to better manage trading risks.

According to RHB Research, immediate support is seen at 24,200 points, followed by 23,500 points. On the upside, the first resistance level is pegged at 25,300 points, with the next target at 26,000 points.

While acknowledging the possibility of increased selling pressure, RHB Research said the prevailing technical indicators continue to support a positive trading bias for HSIF.

data.title: Asian Equities Tumble As Brent Surge Revives Fed Rate Hike Bets
data.description:
Asian shares fell on Friday as a fresh surge in oil prices reignited concerns over inflation, putting pressure on bond markets and increasing expectations that central banks may maintain a more hawkish stance.

MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan declined 1%, while Japan’s Nikkei dropped 2.9% and South Korea’s KOSPI fell 3.7% as investors assessed the impact of escalating Middle East tensions on energy supplies and interest rates.

Brent crude traded at US$100.85 a barrel after jumping 7% overnight to a two-month high of US$102, following attacks by Iran-aligned Houthis on Saudi tankers in the Red Sea. The disruption added pressure on global supply routes, alongside concerns over restrictions around the Strait of Hormuz.

The latest developments came after the collapse of a temporary ceasefire, with the US military launching air strikes on Iran while Tehran responded with attacks on neighbouring countries hosting US bases. Brent has now climbed nearly 40% this month.

“Two of the world’s busiest shipping corridors are under threat in the same month, and markets are only just beginning to work out what that means,” said Nigel Green, CEO of deVere Group.

“With that ceasefire now collapsed and oil back above $100, the drop which gave the Fed room to relax may already be reversing … This looks less like a short-lived spike and more like a genuine reopening of the inflation question,” he added.

Higher oil prices and renewed inflation risks pushed investors to reassess monetary policy expectations, with markets pricing in a one-in-three chance of a US Federal Reserve rate hike as early as next week.

US Treasury yields climbed, with the 10-year yield holding near 4.70% after reaching an 18-month high, while the dollar strengthened. The yen weakened further to 163.89 per US dollar, remaining near a 40-year low.

“Against the backdrop of surging energy prices, the hawkish Fed repricing, and the yen’s loss of safe-haven status, any comments from Japanese officials today about being ready to intervene or faster BoJ rate hike will likely be ignored,” said Tony Sycamore, an analyst at IG.

data.title: Selling Pressure Keeps Bursa In The Red At Midday
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia remained in negative territory at midday on Friday, with the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) falling 9.18 points, or 0.54 per cent, to 1,705.41.

The benchmark index moved between an intraday high of 1,710.80 and a low of 1,701.41 during the morning session.

Broader market performance was also weaker. The FBM Emas Index shed 55.42 points (0.44 per cent) to 12,602.31, while the FBM 70 Index declined 24.88 points (0.14 per cent) to 17,842.47.

The FBM Shariah Index eased 42.21 points (0.34 per cent) to 12,454.07, while the F4GBM Index slipped 4.59 points (0.45 per cent) to 1,022.89.

Among the most actively traded counters, AIMAX was unchanged at 1.0 sen with 281.60 million shares traded, Zetrix fell 2.0 sen to 69.0 sen, and Aimflex eased 0.5 sen to 13.0 sen.

Trive Property Group gained 0.5 sen to 2.0 sen, while VS Industry lost 1.0 sen to 24.0 sen.

On the broader market, eSystems topped the gainers, rising 44 sen to 55 sen, followed by Ideal United Bintang and Kuala Lumpur Kepong (KLK), which each added 24 sen to RM3.99 and RM20.90, respectively.

Meanwhile, Malaysian Pacific Industries (MPI) led the losers, declining RM1.04 to RM45.14, followed by Nestlé (Malaysia), which dropped 72 sen to RM90.48, and UMS Integration, down 26 sen to RM7.79.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Slides At Close, FBM KLCI Settles At 1,701.02
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Friday, with the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) shedding 13.57 points, or 0.79 per cent, to close at 1,701.02 amid broad-based selling pressure.

The benchmark index traded between an intraday high of 1,710.80 and a low of 1,697.76.

The broader market was mostly weaker, with the FBM Emas Index declining 68.19 points, or 0.54 per cent, to 12,589.54. The FBM Shariah Index slipped 52.03 points, or 0.42 per cent, to 12,444.25, while the F4GBM Index eased 5.83 points, or 0.57 per cent, to 1,021.65.

In contrast, the FBM70 Index bucked the broader market trend, rising 23.48 points, or 0.13 per cent, to 17,890.83.

Among the most actively traded stocks, AIMAX was unchanged at 1.0 sen with 285.16 million shares traded, HHRG fell three sen to 15.0 sen, Zetrix slipped one sen to 70.0 sen, Aimflex lost one sen to 12.5 sen, while Trive Property Group gained 0.5 sen to 2.0 sen.

On the broader market, eSystems emerged as the top gainer, surging 44 sen to 55 sen, followed by United Plantations, which added 32 sen to RM33.42, and Allianz Malaysia, up 28 sen to RM21.24.

Malaysian Pacific Industries led the losers, falling RM1.10 to RM45.08, followed by Nestlé (Malaysia), which shed 58 sen to RM90.62, Hong Leong Bank, down 38 sen to RM21.98, Fraser & Neave Holdings, which declined 34 sen to RM28.30, and Press Metal Aluminium Holdings, lower by 19 sen at RM7.86.

data.title: Nikkei Tumbles 3% As Tech Stocks Drag Index Down
data.description:
Japan’s benchmark Nikkei stock index briefly tumbled over 3 percent on Friday morning, dragged down by losses in technology shares, while an escalation in the Middle East conflict also pressured the market.

At 11 a.m. local time, the 225-issue Nikkei Stock Average had fallen 2,007.52 points, or 3.02 percent, from Thursday to 64,415.08. The broader Topix index, meanwhile, was down 50.52 points, or 1.25 percent, at 4,003.36.

The Nikkei’s slide tracked overnight losses on Wall Street, while the escalation in the Middle East conflict, which drove up crude oil futures, also weighed on the market.

data.title: Bitcoin Holds Above US$65,000 As Crypto Market Cap Stays At US$2.22 Trillion
data.description:
Bitcoin continued to trade above the US$65,000 level on Friday as the broader cryptocurrency market remained relatively stable, with investors balancing renewed buying interest against cautious market sentiment.

According to CoinMarketCap data, Bitcoin was trading at US$65,400.91, gaining 0.29% over the past 24 hours and rising 4.41% over the past seven days.

The world’s largest cryptocurrency recorded a market capitalisation of US$1.31 trillion, with 24-hour trading volume reaching US$24.53 billion. Bitcoin’s dominance stood at 58.9% of the total cryptocurrency market.

The overall crypto market capitalisation stood at US$2.22 trillion, down 0.55% over the last 24 hours, while total trading volume reached US$60.92 billion.

Market sentiment remained cautious, with the Crypto Fear & Greed Index registering a reading of 37, indicating “Fear” among investors.

Ethereum outperformed Bitcoin on a daily basis, rising 1.69% over the past 24 hours to US$1,890.75. The second-largest cryptocurrency recorded a market capitalisation of US$228.18 billion, with weekly gains of 3.83%.

Among other major cryptocurrencies, XRP climbed 1.95% over the past 24 hours to US$1.11, while Solana also gained 1.95% to US$75.84. BNB rose 0.43% to US$568.47, while TRON increased 1.05% to US$0.3308.

Several altcoins recorded stronger weekly performances. Monero gained 9.36% over seven days to US$357.02, while Hedera rose 7.37% to US$0.07074. Cardano increased 5.98% to US$0.1679, while Litecoin gained 5.61% to US$46.73.

Despite Bitcoin’s recent recovery, the cryptocurrency market remained below previous highs as investors continued to monitor global economic conditions, interest rate expectations and broader risk appetite.

Stablecoins continued to dominate trading activity, with Tether recording a market capitalisation of US$184.03 billion and 24-hour trading volume of US$51.63 billion. USDC followed with a market capitalisation of US$72.99 billion.

data.title: Asian Chipmakers Rally As Alphabet Raises AI Spending Plans
data.description:
Asian equities advanced on Thursday after major US technology companies reaffirmed aggressive spending on artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure, lifting regional chipmakers, while escalating conflict in the Middle East pushed oil prices to their highest level in six weeks.

Investor sentiment was buoyed after Alphabet and Tesla reported earnings that reinforced expectations of sustained AI infrastructure investment, with Alphabet raising its capital expenditure forecast for 2026.

The increased spending is expected to benefit Asian semiconductor manufacturers, with South Korea’s KOSPI surging more than 3% on gains in SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics, while Japan’s Nikkei climbed 0.7%.

MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan gained about 1%, putting it on track for a 3% weekly gain and snapping a two-week losing streak.

Gary Tan, portfolio manager at Allspring Global Investments, said stronger cloud growth validates higher AI capital expenditure and reinforces that the hyperscaler investment cycle still has momentum.

However, analysts cautioned that investors are increasingly focused on whether the enormous AI spending by US technology giants will translate into meaningful returns.

“For markets, this is not an AI-demand problem, it is an AI-return problem,” said Charu Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo in Singapore.

She noted that while US megacap technology firms bear the cost of AI investments, chipmakers, memory suppliers and infrastructure companies are likely to benefit earlier in the investment cycle.

Meanwhile, European stock futures pointed to a subdued open ahead of the European Central Bank’s policy decision later on Thursday. The ECB is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged while retaining the option of raising rates in September amid heightened geopolitical risks.

Nasdaq futures edged down 0.1% as investors awaited earnings from Microsoft, Meta Platforms and Amazon next week.

Oil prices extended gains after the US launched another round of strikes against Iran and Yemen’s Houthi forces targeted oil tankers in the Red Sea, intensifying concerns over supply disruptions.

Brent crude rose 2% to US$96 a barrel, its highest level in six weeks.

Analysts warned that any disruption to shipping through both the Strait of Hormuz and the Bab el-Mandeb Strait could affect more than a quarter of global oil and gas supplies, raising concerns over inflation and global economic growth.

“The worries about global growth are well-placed, we think,” said Thierry Wizman, global foreign exchange and rates strategist at Macquarie Group.

Higher oil prices also fuelled expectations that the US Federal Reserve could tighten monetary policy further, with traders pricing in 42 basis points of rate hikes this year and a September increase now fully priced.

In currency markets, the Japanese yen traded at 163.07 against the US dollar after Bloomberg reported that Bank of Japan officials were open to a faster pace of rate hikes.

The currency had weakened to 163.24 earlier this week, its lowest level since December 1986, keeping traders alert for possible intervention by Japanese authorities.

OCBC strategists said while foreign exchange intervention could slow the yen’s decline, a more lasting recovery would likely require the Bank of Japan to accelerate interest rate increases.

Reuters

data.title: Euro Nears One-Week High Ahead Of ECB Decision As Oil Risks Cloud Inflation Outlook
data.description:
The euro climbed towards a one-week high on Thursday as investors awaited the European Central Bank’s (ECB) policy decision, with markets expecting policymakers to leave interest rates unchanged while signalling the possibility of another hike later this year amid rising energy prices.

The single currency rose 0.17% to US$1.1429, while the US dollar index, which tracks the greenback against a basket of major currencies, eased 0.13% to 100.98.

Although the ECB raised interest rates in June and indicated further tightening could follow, recent data showing easing inflation, slower wage growth and softer economic activity have reduced expectations of an immediate follow-up increase.

However, escalating tensions in the Middle East have pushed oil prices to their highest level in more than six weeks, raising concerns that higher energy costs could reignite inflationary pressures across the eurozone.

“We have flagged a small risk that the ECB decides to deliver an early hike,” said Samara Hammoud, international economist and currency strategist at Commonwealth Bank of Australia. “A ‘hawkish hold’ is another upside risk to EUR/USD today.”

Brent crude extended gains after the US launched fresh strikes against Iran while Iran-backed Houthi forces claimed attacks on oil tankers in the Red Sea, adding to fears of supply disruptions and complicating the inflation outlook for central banks.

Elsewhere, commodity-linked currencies also strengthened, with the Australian dollar rising 0.23% to US$0.7012 and the New Zealand dollar trading at US$0.5818. Sterling gained nearly 0.1% to US$1.3384.

In cryptocurrency markets, Bitcoin slipped 0.5% to US$65,557.35, while Ether fell 0.44% to US$1,917.65.

The Japanese yen showed little sign of recovery, edging just 0.04% higher to 163.05 against the US dollar after touching 163.23 earlier this week, its weakest level since December 1986.

A Reuters survey published on Thursday found that 86% of economists expect the Bank of Japan (BOJ) to raise interest rates again this year, potentially as early as October, although 95% believe rates will remain unchanged this quarter.

Japan’s Finance Minister reiterated on Thursday that authorities stand ready to take decisive action in the foreign exchange market if necessary, following repeated verbal interventions as the yen remains under pressure.

Tokyo previously conducted yen-buying interventions in April and May after the currency weakened beyond the 160-per-dollar level.

The Reuters poll also showed nearly 80% of economists believe the yen’s current level around 160 per US dollar is weaker than justified by Japan’s economic fundamentals.

Kumiko Ishikawa, senior foreign exchange market analyst at Sony Financial Group, said the dollar’s move above the 163 level has heightened market expectations of intervention, although the absence of stronger warnings from Japanese officials has left traders uncertain over when authorities might step in.

Reuters

data.title: Sector Rotation Shapes Mixed Session with Utilities in Focus
data.description:
The stock market ended the trading session on a mixed note, with gains in utilities, construction, and selected plantation-related counters offset by selling pressure in semiconductor and heavyweight plantation stocks.

At the closing bell, sentiment remained cautious as investors rotated into infrastructure-linked and defensive names, while profit-taking in recent outperformers weighed on technology and consumer counters.

Leading the gainers was Panasonic Manufacturing Malaysia Bhd, which rose 33 sen to RM5.99 on 230,900 shares traded. Ornapaper Bhd followed, advancing 22 sen to 87 sen on 21,300 shares.

Tenaga Nasional Bhd also gained 22 sen to RM14.50 on heavy turnover of 7,400,700 shares, supported by renewed interest in utility stocks. Sarawak Plantation Bhd climbed 17 sen to RM4.49 on 472,700 shares, while Sunway Construction Group Bhd added 15 sen to RM7.73 on 3,437,200 shares, reflecting continued interest in construction-related counters.

On the downside, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd led the decliners, falling RM1.58 to RM46.18 on 139,900 shares, as semiconductor stocks faced profit-taking after recent gains. United Plantations Bhd slipped 94 sen to RM33.10 on 966,600 shares, tracking weakness in plantation counters.

Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd eased 32 sen to RM91.20 on 73,700 shares, while Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd declined 26 sen to RM20.66 on 154,000 shares. Malayan Cement Bhd fell 19 sen to RM6.14 on heavy trading of 4,021,000 shares.

Market analysts noted that the session reflected sector rotation, with investors shifting towards utilities and construction counters while locking in gains in technology and plantation names. Overall market activity remained selective, with high-volume trading concentrated in key industrial and infrastructure-related stocks.

data.title: Gold Eyes US$4,200 Resistance As Investors Track Fed Outlook
data.description:
Gold prices eased on Thursday after reaching a two-week high in the previous session, as rising oil prices linked to escalating Middle East tensions increased inflation concerns and raised expectations of a more cautious stance from the US Federal Reserve.

Spot gold fell 0.6% to US$4,103.39 per ounce by 0713 GMT, after climbing to US$4,165.87 on Wednesday, its highest level since July 7. US gold futures for August delivery also declined 1.1% to US$4,106.40.

The decline came as crude oil prices climbed to their highest level in more than six weeks following renewed military tensions involving the US, Iran and Yemen’s Houthis, which raised concerns over potential disruptions to global energy supplies.

Jigar Trivedi, senior research analyst at IndusInd Securities, said higher oil prices were adding pressure on inflation expectations and increasing the likelihood of future US rate hikes, limiting gold’s upside momentum.

The US dollar weakened 0.1%, making dollar-denominated gold more affordable for holders of other currencies. However, two-year US Treasury yields climbed to a 17-month high as investors assessed the impact of higher energy prices on inflation.

Markets are now focused on the Federal Reserve’s policy meeting next week, with the central bank widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged. Futures markets are pricing in a 77% chance of a rate increase in September, according to the CME FedWatch Tool.

Meanwhile, RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) noted that gold’s bullish momentum has strengthened after COMEX gold surged US$75.50 to close at US$4,151.90 in the previous session.

RHB Research said the commodity has been forming a series of higher lows, signalling improving momentum, with immediate support emerging at US$4,000 and resistance seen at US$4,200.

However, the research house maintained a bearish trading bias, expecting stronger resistance at US$4,400. RHB Research said it would retain its short position initiated at US$4,605.70, with a stop-loss level set at US$4,400.

The research house identified US$3,850 as the next downside support level if gold fails to hold above the US$4,000 mark.

data.title: Gold Slips Below US$4,100 As Fed Rate Hike Bets Return, RHB Sees US$4,000 Test
data.description:
Gold prices edged lower on Friday as rising expectations of higher US interest rates weighed on the precious metal, while renewed tensions in the Middle East pushed oil prices back above US$100 a barrel and revived inflation concerns.

Spot gold fell 0.2% to US$4,037.29 per ounce by 0702 GMT, following a 2% decline in the previous session. US gold futures for August delivery also eased 0.3% to US$4,039.80 per ounce.

Despite the recent weakness, bullion remained on track for a small weekly gain of around 0.5%, although prices were more than US$130 below the two-week high recorded on Wednesday.

GoldSilver Central Managing Director Brian Lan said gold could continue to experience volatility in the near term as prices remain trapped within a broad range.

“In the short term, we expect more volatility… gold has been trading between $3,980 all the way to about $4,170 and has been stuck in this way for weeks. Every time prices hit close to $4,000 or slightly below, we see that there will be big buyers coming to buy it back up,” he said.

The pressure on gold came after Brent crude surged 7% on Thursday, breaking above US$100 a barrel for the first time since May following attacks by Iran-aligned Houthi fighters on two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea.

Higher oil prices have raised concerns over another inflationary shock, increasing the possibility that central banks may maintain higher interest rates for longer. While gold is traditionally viewed as a hedge against inflation, higher interest rates tend to reduce demand for the non-yielding asset.

Investors are also watching the Federal Reserve’s policy meeting next week, with traders pricing in an 81% chance of a rate hike in September, according to the CME FedWatch Tool.

RHB Sees Gold Retesting US$4,000 Support

Meanwhile, RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) maintained a negative trading bias on COMEX Gold, saying the commodity is likely to continue its correction after facing strong selling pressure.

RHB Research noted that COMEX Gold plunged US$101.70 in the previous session to close at US$4,050.20, after opening at US$4,126 and reaching an intraday high of US$4,144 before falling to a low of US$4,042.50.

The research house said the latest bearish price action, coupled with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) falling below the 50% mark, suggested that negative momentum was strengthening.

RHB Research expects gold to fall below the key US$4,000 support level and extend its correction towards US$3,850. The research house identified US$4,200 as the immediate resistance level, followed by US$4,400.

“We maintain the negative trading bias,” RHB Research said.

The research house recommended traders retain their short position initiated at US$4,605.70, based on the close on March 19, with a stop-loss level set at US$4,400 to manage risks.

Elsewhere in the precious metals market, spot silver rose 0.1% to US$57.76 per ounce and was on track for a weekly gain of 3.4%. Platinum declined 1% to US$1,583.91 while palladium dropped 1.7% to US$1,236.16, with both metals heading for weekly losses.

data.title: Stock Picks: Gamuda And CTOS Digital
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Gamuda Bhd and CTOS Digital Bhd as its latest technical stock picks, citing renewed bullish momentum following key breakout levels.

The research house said Gamuda has resumed its upward movement after breaking above the RM4.30 resistance level with a fresh bullish candlestick. The stock also climbed above its 21-day simple moving average, signalling that the positive technical setup remains intact.

RHB Research expects Gamuda to test the next resistance at RM4.45, followed by RM4.60 if buying momentum continues. However, it said a decline below the RM4.15 support level would weaken market sentiment and signal a resumption of the correction.

Meanwhile, CTOS Digital is expected to extend its positive price action after staging an upside breakout above the RM0.68 resistance level.

According to RHB Research, the renewed bullish momentum could see the stock test RM0.71 before advancing towards RM0.74.

The research house noted that the bullish outlook would be invalidated should CTOS Digital fall below its RM0.65 support level.

data.title: FBM KLCI Ends Higher On Selective Buying Despite Mixed Broader Market
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia closed on a mixed note on Wednesday, with the benchmark FBM KLCI rising on selective buying in heavyweight stocks, while broader market indices ended mostly lower.

The FBM KLCI gained 3.22 points, or 0.19 per cent, to close at 1,714.59.

However, the broader market was weaker, with the FBM70 Index falling 89.46 points, or 0.50 per cent, to 17,867.35. The F4GBM Index edged up 0.52 points to 1,027.48, while the FBM Emas Index added 1.77 points to 12,657.73. The FBM Shariah Index slipped 1.41 points to 12,496.28.

Among the most actively traded counters, AIMAX Bhd led the market with 3.30 billion shares changing hands, closing 0.5 sen lower at 1.0 sen.

Zetrix AI Bhd was the second most active, easing 0.5 sen to 71.0 sen with 1.21 billion shares traded.

Among other active stocks are Tanco Holdings Bhd,VS Industry Bhd, and Pegasus Heights Bhd.

The benchmark index’s modest gain contrasted with the softer performance across broader market gauges, reflecting continued selective buying amid cautious investor sentiment.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Opens Lower As FBM KLCI Slips 0.4% Amid Cautious Sentiment
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened lower on Friday, tracking weaker global market sentiment, with the benchmark FBM KLCI falling 6.94 points or 0.40% to 1,707.65 as at 9.20am.

The index opened at 1,710.45 before moving between an intraday high of 1,710.80 and a low of 1,706.67. The broader market also traded in negative territory, with the FBM Top 100 Index declining 44.30 points or 0.35% to 12,437.56.

Market sentiment remained cautious following overnight losses on Wall Street, where major US indices ended lower as investors weighed concerns over artificial intelligence spending, rising oil prices and renewed inflation pressures.

The FBM 70 Index eased 36.70 points or 0.21% to 17,830.65, while the FBM Small Cap Index declined 24.05 points or 0.15% to 15,746.71. The FBM EMAS Index slipped 41.01 points or 0.32% to 12,616.72.

Among sector indices, Energy was the only major sector trading higher in early trade, gaining 1.54 points or 0.20% to 775.68. Construction fell 1.03 points or 0.36% to 288.18, while Consumer Products & Services dropped 1.48 points or 0.30% to 494.64.

Trading activity was led by AIMax Holdings Bhd, which was the most active counter with 2.12 billion shares traded, rising 0.5 sen or 50% to 1.5 sen.

Other actively traded counters included VS Industry Bhd, which declined 1 sen to 24 sen with 211.47 million shares traded, while Dayang Enterprise Holdings Bhd (ARMADA) gained 0.5 sen to 37 sen with 102.11 million shares traded.

Among top gainers, Esys Holdings Bhd surged 44 sen or 400% to 55 sen, making it the strongest performer in early trading following its debut on the LEAP Market. The stock opened at 15 sen and climbed to a high of 55 sen.

Other gainers included Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd, which added 10 sen to RM20.76, while Petronas Gas Bhd and Westports Holdings Bhd each gained 6 sen to RM17.56 and RM6.75 respectively.

On the losing side, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd was the biggest decliner, falling 68 sen to RM45.50. Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd dropped 36 sen to RM90.84, while UMS Integration Ltd declined 26 sen to RM7.79.

The market opened as investors continued to monitor global developments, including higher crude oil prices and expectations surrounding the upcoming US Federal Reserve policy meeting.

data.title: Blue-Chip Support Cushions Market As Semiconductor Counters Slide
data.description:
The stock market ended the trading session on a mixed note, with selective buying in plantation and blue-chip counters offset by weakness in semiconductor, consumer, and industrial stocks.

At the closing bell, sentiment remained cautious as investors rotated into selected gainers, while continued profit-taking in recent outperformers weighed on market momentum.

Leading the gainers was ESYS Holdings Bhd, which surged 44 sen to 55 sen on 67,400 shares traded, driven by strong buying interest despite relatively light trading activity. United Plantations Bhd followed, gaining 32 sen to RM33.42 on 127,100 shares as plantation counters attracted selective support.

Allianz Malaysia Bhd advanced 28 sen to RM21.24 on 36,400 shares, while Chin Teck Plantations Bhd added 28 sen to RM11.34 on 17,300 shares. Ideal Capital Bhd also rose 24 sen to RM3.99, although trading volume remained minimal.

On the downside, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd led the decliners, falling RM1.10 to RM45.08 on 121,500 shares, as semiconductor counters remained under selling pressure. Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd declined 58 sen to RM90.62 on 81,800 shares, extending weakness among consumer staples.

Hong Leong Bank Bhd slipped 38 sen to RM21.98 on 729,500 shares, reflecting mild selling pressure in banking heavyweights. Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd eased 34 sen to RM28.30 on 40,700 shares, while Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd dropped 19 sen to RM7.86 on heavy turnover of 7,818,300 shares.

Market analysts noted that trading remained highly selective, with plantation and selected blue-chip counters attracting interest while technology and consumer stocks faced profit-taking. Overall sentiment stayed cautious, with investors closely monitoring sector rotation and earnings-related developments.

data.title: SGX Opens Lower As STI Falls 0.7%, Investors Track Global Market Weakness
data.description:
Singapore shares opened lower on Friday, with the benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) declining 39.24 points or 0.7% to 5,542.52 as investors monitored weaker global sentiment.

Market breadth remained negative in early trading, with 85 gainers compared with 191 decliners. Trading activity stood at 132.40 million shares valued at S$238.04 million as at 9.29am.

Among major index counters, DBS Group Holdings slipped 67 cents or 0.91% to S$72.83, while Singtel traded at S$4.40.

Other key STI constituents including OCBC Bank, ST Engineering and United Overseas Bank were also among the actively watched counters, with OCBC at S$28.58, ST Engineering at S$10.59 and UOB at S$42.71.

The decline followed overnight weakness on Wall Street, where US equities ended lower as investors weighed concerns over artificial intelligence spending, rising oil prices and renewed inflation risks. The Nasdaq Composite fell more than 2%, while the S&P 500 dropped over 1%.

Investors were also tracking higher crude oil prices after Brent futures climbed above US$100 a barrel amid escalating tensions in the Middle East, raising concerns that renewed energy disruptions could keep inflation elevated and influence the Federal Reserve’s interest rate outlook.

In the derivatives market, SGX trading volume stood at 62.69 thousand contracts. FTSE China A50 Index Futures for July 2026 were at 15,171 points with 6,250 contracts traded.

Other futures contracts saw Nikkei 225 Index Futures for September 2026 at 64,665 points, SGX FTSE Taiwan Index Futures for July 2026 at 3,812.75 points, MSCI Singapore Index Futures for July 2026 at 513.15 points and FTSE China H50 Index Futures for July 2026 at 15,722.50 points.

In the currency futures market, USD/SGD Futures for August 2026 were quoted at 1.2895, while USD/CNH Futures for September 2026 stood at 6.7531.
